title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Tue Apr 21, 2026
summary: Islamabad US-Iran talks collapsed after 21 hours on April 21 as US negotiators departed without a deal, with the ceasefire set to expire at midnight April 22. Trump threatened Iran with unprecedented consequences; Iranian parliament speaker vowed 'new cards on the battlefield.' Taliban official Zakir Jalaly publicly urged Iran to halt regional strikes, underscoring Afghanistan's vulnerability via Herat and Nimroz supply corridors. ISIS-K struck a mosque in Mazar-e-Sharif and a target in Kunduz on the same day. The Af-Pak non-escalation framework held for a 12th consecutive day — the longest lull in the conflict — while Urumqi Round 2 is now confirmed for May, delayed by Pakistan's total diplomatic absorption in the Iran crisis.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Mon Apr 20, 2026
summary: US Delegation Arrives in Islamabad for Iran Talks; Tehran Declines; Urumqi Round II Slips Past April.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Sun Apr 19, 2026
summary: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again; Pakistan Mediation Under Acute Strain; Af-Pak Non-Escalation Holds at Day 10.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Sat Apr 18, 2026
summary: The Urumqi non-escalation framework has held for 9 consecutive days — the longest uninterrupted lull since Operation Ghazab-il-Haq began February 26. Pakistan PM Sharif returned from Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and the Antalya Diplomacy Forum while Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir concluded a three-day Tehran visit, both aimed at arranging a second US-Iran peace round before the April 22 ceasefire expiry. No date was confirmed for US-Iran Round 2 but Pakistan maintained active mediation channels. The second round of China-mediated Af-Pak Urumqi talks remains expected by end of April. Five gap-day event files covering April 13–18 have been added, along with political profile for Field Marshal Asim Munir and updated KPI tracking the non-escalation period.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Fri Apr 17, 2026
summary: Pakistan PM at Antalya Diplomacy Forum; Af-Pak Border Quiet Under Urumqi Framework.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Thu Apr 16, 2026
summary: White House Calls Pakistan 'Sole Mediator' as Munir Meets Iranian Leadership in Tehran.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Wed Apr 15, 2026
summary: Pakistan launched parallel diplomatic missions on April 15 aimed at arranging a second round of US-Iran peace talks.

title: Breaking: Pakistan PM travels to Saudi Arabia and Turkey amid prospect of new US-Iran talk

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Mon Apr 13, 2026
summary: Pakistan launched an intense diplomatic push on April 13 to revive US-Iran peace negotiations following the failure of the Islamabad round (April 11–12).

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Sun Apr 12, 2026
summary: The high-stakes US-Iran peace talks hosted by Pakistan in Islamabad ended on April 12 without an agreement.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Sat Apr 11, 2026
summary: Direct US-Iran peace talks began in Islamabad on April 11, with the Af-Pak conflict in a fragile non-escalation pause.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Fri Apr 10, 2026
summary: The Diplomat: China's Post-Urumqi Framework Sets Stage for End-of-April Talks; Analysts Assess Prospects.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Update — Apr 09, 2026
summary: Afghanistan and Pakistan agreed to explore a 'comprehensive solution' to their border conflict after China-mediated talks in Urumqi — the most significant diplomatic development since the conflict began in February 2026. Afghan Taliban labeled the seven-day talks 'useful' on April 7; China formalized the framework on April 8. However, Pakistani artillery struck Asadabad, capital of Kunar province, on April 8, killing 1 civilian and injuring 16 even as peace talks continued. UN OCHA warned that ~100,000 people in Nuristan's Bargi Matal and Kamdesh districts lacked humanitarian access. A new map point was added for the Asadabad strike, and a contested claim on the Urumqi talks' significance was added to the diplomatic tracker.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Wed Apr 8, 2026
summary: China's foreign ministry announced that Afghanistan and Pakistan had agreed to explore a 'comprehensive solution' to their armed conflict following the Urumqi talks. Pakistani artillery struck Asadabad, the provincial capital of Kunar province, killing at least one Afghan civilian and injuring 16 others.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Tue Apr 7, 2026
summary: After seven days of China-mediated negotiations in Urumqi, Afghanistan's Taliban government labeled the talks 'useful' and expressed cautious optimism about resolving the conflict with Pakistan. UN OCHA: ~100,000 in Nuristan's Bargi Matal and Kamdesh Districts Cut Off from Humanitarian Aid.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Update — Apr 06, 2026
summary: Taliban and TTP fighters launched a coordinated ground assault on a Pakistani border post in Ghulam Khan Sector (North Waziristan) that was repelled, killing 37 attackers and wounding 80+. A separate TTP border infiltration attempt was foiled the same night with 8 militants killed. Pakistan announced cumulative Operation Ghazab-il-Haq toll of 796 Taliban killed and 1,043 wounded, figures hotly disputed by Kabul. Pakistan formally rejected UN human rights experts' claim of 'no credible evidence' linking TTP attacks to Afghan soil, citing multiple UN Security Council monitoring reports and SIGAR data. China-mediated peace talks in Urumqi opened April 2 with mid-level delegations from both sides — but Pakistani artillery and drone strikes on Kunar, Paktika, and Khost continued simultaneously, killing 2 children and wounding ~30 civilians and firing over 500 artillery shells into Kunar alone between April 2-4.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Sun Apr 5, 2026
summary: Pakistan Formally Rejects UN Experts' Claim of 'No Credible Evidence' for TTP Attacks from Afghan Soil. Pakistan Announces Cumulative Operation Toll: 796 Taliban Killed, 286 Checkposts Destroyed.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Sat Apr 4, 2026
summary: Pakistani Medical Students Stage Protest at Torkham, Stranded from Afghan Universities. Pakistani forces fired 185 long-range artillery shells into a single district in Kunar province, wounding 10 people.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Fri Apr 3, 2026
summary: China's government confirmed that Pakistan-Afghanistan peace talks in Urumqi were 'advancing', with both sides engaging in substantive discussions. Afghan Economy Minister: 27,000+ Families Displaced by Pakistan Operations Across Nine Provinces. PICSS: Pakistan Records 35% Drop in Terrorist Fatalities in March as Op Ghazab Delivers Security Gains.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Thu Apr 2, 2026
summary: Senior officials from Pakistan and Afghanistan convened in Urumqi, northwestern China, for the first formal round of ceasefire talks since the post-Ramadan truce expired. Pakistan launched sustained mortar, rocket, and drone strikes across three eastern Afghan provinces — Kunar, Paktika, and Khost — on April 2. Pakistan partially reopened the Torkham border crossing on April 2 for the limited purpose of repatriating detained Afghan nationals.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Update — Apr 01, 2026
summary: Pakistan formally announced the full resumption of Operation Ghazab-il-Haq on March 31, declaring the campaign would continue 'until its objectives are achieved.' On March 30, the Pakistani Air Force conducted its widest single-day strike package of the conflict, hitting 41 locations including Taliban brigade and battalion headquarters in Nangarhar and an army HQ in Kandahar, killing 1 and wounding 16 civilians according to Afghan officials. UN Human Rights Chief Türk issued a formal condemnation calling the conflict a situation that 'piles misery on misery' and pleading for dialogue. The Torkham border crossing partially reopened on March 31 for Afghan national returns only, while trade and passenger services remained suspended. By April 1, the conflict had entered its fifth week with no ceasefire framework in sight, drawing CFR analysis describing it as a 'low-intensity interstate war' unlikely to resolve through negotiation in the near term.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Tue Mar 31, 2026
summary: Pakistan Formally Announces Resumption of Operation Ghazab-il-Haq. Pakistan partially reopened the Torkham border crossing on March 31 to allow Afghan nationals to return to Afghanistan, following its closure since the Afghan sniper incident on March 27.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Mon Mar 30, 2026
summary: Pakistani Air Force jets struck 41 locations across Afghanistan on March 30, targeting brigade and battalion headquarters in Nangarhar province and an army headquarters in Kandahar province. UN Human Rights Chief Türk: Af-Pak Conflict 'Piles Misery on Misery', Pleads for Dialogue.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: Pakistan formally resumed Operation Ghazab-il-Haq on March 26 following the Eid ceasefire collapse, with artillery strikes continuing in Kunar, Khost, Paktika and Nangarhar provinces. Human Rights Watch declared Pakistan's March 16 airstrike on the Omid Drug Rehabilitation Center in Kabul unlawful and a possible war crime, as the revised death toll reached 411 killed. A mass funeral was held in Kabul on March 26 with Interior Minister Haqqani vowing revenge. The Torkham border crossing briefly reopened for deportations on March 26 before being closed again after an Afghan sniper wounded a Pakistani Frontier Corps officer on the night of March 27. UNHCR confirmed 115,000 Afghans have been displaced since February 26, with no new ceasefire framework in sight.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Sat Mar 28, 2026
summary: The Torkham border crossing was fully closed on March 28 following the previous night's sniper attack that wounded a Pakistani Frontier Corps Subedar. UNHCR reported as of late March that at least 115,000 Afghans had been displaced since Pakistan launched Operation Ghazab-il-Haq on February 26.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Fri Mar 27, 2026
summary: Human Rights Watch: Pakistan's March 16 Omid Center Airstrike Was Unlawful and Possibly a War Crime. Late on the evening of March 27, at approximately 9:30 PM local time, a Frontier Corps Subedar was shot by a sniper from the Afghan side at the Awal Khan post near Torkham crossing. BBC Satellite Report: Pakistan Has Fenced 32 Sq Km of Afghan Territory; Paktika Occupation Claims Disputed. Pakistan Claims 527 Taliban Fighters Killed; Taliban Claims 307 Pakistani Soldiers Dead.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: The five-day Eid al-Fitr ceasefire between Pakistan and Afghanistan, brokered by Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Turkey, collapsed on March 24 as cross-border artillery exchanges resumed within minutes of its expiry in Kunar and Zabul provinces, killing at least two Afghan civilians. Pakistan's Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar signaled no change in strategic posture, reaffirming commitment to 'eradicate terrorism' and leaving the door open to future military operations. The TTP simultaneously ended its own three-day Eid ceasefire, resuming attacks inside Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. The UN has documented at least 289 civilian deaths in Afghanistan and 115,000 displaced since fighting intensified on February 26, following Pakistan's retaliatory airstrikes — including the March 16 strike on the Omid drug rehabilitation center in Kabul that killed at least 143 people. No new mediation framework is currently active.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Wed Mar 25, 2026
summary: Clashes Resume in Zabul and Kunar as Pakistan Vows to 'Eradicate Terrorism'. Pakistan FM: 'Firmly Committed to Eradicate Terrorism' — No De-escalation Signal.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Tue Mar 24, 2026
summary: The five-day Eid al-Fitr ceasefire between Pakistan and Afghanistan expired at midnight on March 24. The Tehrik-e-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) announced it had resumed attacks inside Pakistan following the conclusion of its three-day Eid ceasefire.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Mon Mar 23, 2026
summary: 22 Afghan-Pakistani Clerics Jointly Urge Ceasefire Extension; Truce Ends at Midnight. UN Experts Warn of Imminent Ceasefire Collapse, Call for Permanent Peace Process.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Sun Mar 22, 2026
summary: The five-day Eid al-Fitr ceasefire declared by Pakistan on March 18 remained nominally in force on March 22, though under significant strain. Satellite Imagery Confirms Omid Drug Rehabilitation Center Destroyed by March 16 Strike.

title: Afghanistan-Pakistan War Tracker Update — Thu Mar 19, 2026
summary: A five-day Eid al-Fitr ceasefire between Pakistan and Afghanistan came into effect at midnight on March 19, brokered at the request of Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Turkey. The Pakistani Taliban (Tehrik-e-Taliban Pakistan, TTP) announced a three-day Eid ceasefire, hours after Pakistan and Afghanistan declared their own temporary pause in fighting. UN: 115,000 Afghans Displaced, 289 Civilians Killed Since Feb 26.
